name: Access Technologies Inc. (ATI)
description: Nonprofit providing assistive technology training, assessment, and demonstration for individuals with disabilities, including computer access, communication devices, and adaptive equipment.

name: Albertina Kerr
description: Nonprofit organization providing services for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, including residential programs, employment services, and crisis support.

name: Autism Society of Oregon
description: State chapter providing information, support, and advocacy for individuals with autism and their families, including support groups, events, and resource navigation.

name: Beaverton Inclusive Playground (Howard M. Terpenning Recreation Complex)
description: Fully accessible and inclusive playground with adaptive equipment, sensory play areas, and wheelchair-accessible surfaces in Beaverton.

name: Camp Meadowood Springs
description: Summer residential camp for children with communication disorders, learning differences, and autism, providing speech-language therapy in a camp setting.

name: Easter Seals Oregon
description: Provides disability services including early childhood programs, therapy services, and support for individuals and families living with disabilities.

name: FACT Oregon
description: Family advocacy, training, and support organization helping families of children and adults with disabilities navigate systems, understand their rights, and connect with resources.

name: FACT Oregon (Family and Community Together)
description: Oregon's Statewide Parent Training and Information Center for families experiencing disability. Personalized special education support, IEP help, and disability advocacy from childhood through adulthood. On-demand trainings in multiple languages (Arabic, Chinese, Russian, Somali, Spanish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese).

name: Harper's Playground
description: Nonprofit dedicated to building universally accessible playgrounds that bring together people of all abilities. Arbor Lodge Park playground in Portland is their flagship project.

name: Independent Living Resources (ILR)
description: Center for independent living providing advocacy, peer mentoring, skills training, and information and referral for people with all types of disabilities.

name: Mt. Hood Kiwanis Camp
description: Year-round camp and outdoor recreation programs for children and adults with disabilities, including summer camps, winter sports, and respite weekends.

name: Multnomah County Developmental Disabilities Division
description: County services for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities including service coordination, community inclusion, and family support.

name: NAMI Oregon
description: Oregon chapter of the National Alliance on Mental Illness providing mental health support, education, and advocacy including support groups for individuals and families.

name: Northwest Access Fund
description: Nonprofit providing affordable loans and financial education for assistive technology, home modifications, and vehicles for people with disabilities in Oregon and Washington.

name: Oregon ABLE Savings Plan
description: Tax-advantaged savings accounts for eligible individuals with disabilities that do not affect eligibility for means-tested benefits like SSI and Medicaid.

name: Oregon Commission for the Blind
description: State agency providing vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, and assistive technology for individuals who are blind or visually impaired.

name: Oregon Council on Developmental Disabilities (OCDD)
description: Federally funded council promoting inclusion, self-determination, and independence for Oregonians with developmental disabilities through systems change and advocacy.

name: Oregon Department of Developmental Disabilities Services (ODDS)
description: State agency providing services for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities, including case management, residential supports, employment services, and community living programs.

name: Oregon Disability Parking Permit
description: Information on applying for disability parking permits through the Oregon DMV for individuals with qualifying disabilities.

name: Oregon Disability Rights (Disability Rights Oregon)
description: Oregon's federally designated protection and advocacy system providing legal advocacy, information, and referral for people with disabilities.

name: Oregon Early Intervention / Early Childhood Special Education (EI/ECSE)
description: Oregon's statewide early intervention program for infants and toddlers (birth to age 3) with developmental delays or disabilities, and early childhood special education for children ages 3 to 5.

name: Oregon Emergency Management - Access and Functional Needs
description: Oregon's emergency preparedness resources for individuals with disabilities and access and functional needs, including evacuation planning and emergency kits.

name: Oregon Family Support Network
description: Peer-run organization supporting families of children and youth with mental health, behavioral, and emotional challenges through support groups and system navigation.

name: Oregon Family to Family Health Information Center (OF2F)
description: Family-led center providing information, education, and support to families of children and youth with special health care needs and disabilities.

name: Oregon Health & Science University (OHSU) - Child Development & Rehabilitation Center (CDRC)
description: Comprehensive center providing diagnosis, treatment, and therapy for children with developmental disabilities, autism, cerebral palsy, and other conditions. Offers multidisciplinary evaluations.

name: Oregon Health Plan (OHP) - Medicaid
description: Oregon's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income individuals and families, including children and adults with disabilities. Covers therapies, equipment, and medical services.

name: Oregon Home Education Network (OHEN) - Special Needs
description: Maintains a community list for parents homeschooling special needs children in Oregon. Connects to Portland-area groups including the PDX Aspergers Network and Aut-2B-Home (for families homeschooling autistic spectrum children).

name: Oregon Housing and Community Services - Disability Housing
description: State resources for accessible and affordable housing, including Section 8 vouchers, supportive housing, and home modification assistance for Oregonians with disabilities.

name: Oregon Office on Disability and Health
description: State office promoting health equity for Oregonians with disabilities through data collection, public health programs, and community partnerships.

name: Oregon Parent Training and Information Center (PTI)
description: Federally funded parent training center providing information and support for families of children with disabilities on special education rights, IEPs, and school advocacy.

name: Oregon Post-Secondary Transition Programs
description: Oregon Department of Education resources for transition planning from school to adult life for students with disabilities ages 16 to 21.

name: Oregon Respite Program (Lifespan Respite)
description: Oregon's respite care program providing temporary relief for family caregivers of individuals with disabilities and special health care needs.
cost: Varies, some funded through Medicaid waivers

name: Oregon School for the Deaf
description: State residential and day school providing bilingual education (ASL and English) for deaf and hard-of-hearing students from birth through age 21.

name: Oregon Technology Access Program (OTAP)
description: Oregon's Assistive Technology Act program providing device demonstrations, short-term loans, and information about assistive technology solutions for people with disabilities.

name: Oregon Vocational Rehabilitation Services (VR)
description: State vocational rehabilitation program helping individuals with disabilities prepare for, find, and maintain employment through job training, education, and support services.

name: Portland Children's Museum - Accessibility
description: Children's museum offering accessible play experiences and sensory-friendly hours for children with disabilities and sensory needs.

name: Portland Parks & Recreation - Adaptive & Inclusive Recreation
description: Inclusive recreation programs for people of all abilities through Portland Parks & Recreation, including adaptive sports, social clubs, day camps, and community events.

name: Portland Sensory-Friendly Events at OMSI
description: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry (OMSI) offers sensory-friendly events with reduced noise, lighting, and crowds for visitors with sensory processing differences.

name: Providence Inclusive Recreation & Adapted Sports
description: Adaptive and inclusive recreation and sports programs for children and adults with disabilities in the Portland metro area.

name: Rebuilding Together Portland
description: Nonprofit providing free home repairs and accessibility modifications, including ramps, grab bars, and widened doorways for low-income homeowners with disabilities.

name: Ride Connection
description: Nonprofit providing accessible transportation options for older adults and people with disabilities in the Portland metro area, including door-to-door rides and travel training.

name: Special Olympics Oregon
description: Year-round sports training and athletic competition for children and adults with intellectual disabilities across Oregon.

name: The Arc Oregon
description: Statewide advocacy organization for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families, providing information, referral, and systems advocacy.

name: TriMet LIFT Paratransit
description: Door-to-door shared ride transit service for people who are unable to use regular TriMet buses or MAX trains due to a disability or disabling health condition.

name: Disability Rights Oregon
description: Oregon's Protection & Advocacy organization providing legal advocacy for people with disabilities. Children's Rights project focuses on special education, foster care, and systemic change.

name: The Arc Portland Metro
description: Local Arc chapter serving Portland metro area, providing advocacy, services, and supports for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families.

name: The Arc of Lane County
description: Local Arc chapter serving Lane County, providing advocacy and community-based services for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: The Arc of Benton County
description: Local Arc chapter serving Benton County, providing supports and services for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
